options.content:
## PostgreSQL / CloudNativePG — read me first

Pick the tier in the **`Tier (namespace)`** variable above. The two tiers differ **by design**, so which panels carry data depends on the tier:

| | **`microservices`** (stable) | **`microservices-develop`** (develop) |
|---|---|---|
| Instances | **3** — HA (1 primary + 2 replicas) | **1** — single, no replicas |
| Backups / WAL archive | **enabled** → GCS (`barmanObjectStore`, daily `ScheduledBackup`) | **none** (lean tier) |
| Full dashboard? | **yes — every section populates** | Replication + Backups sections **empty by design** |

### Why a healthy dashboard shows many `0`s
Most "empty"-looking panels are **health indicators where `0` is the *good* value** — they only go non-zero under a real problem (or under load). Breakdown:

- **🟢 `0` = healthy on *any* tier** (not a fault, don't "fix"): *Switchover required* (No), *Fencing* (Off), *Collector errors* / *Last collection error* (OK), *Deadlocks & conflicts*, *Backends waiting*, *Rollback ratio*, *Block read/I·O time* (= 100% cache hit), *WAL buffers full*, *MXID age*, and on stable *Replication lag / diff bytes* (= replicas keep up, 0 lag).
- **➖ Empty on develop only — single-instance, no replicas:** *Streaming/Sync replicas*, *Replication lag*, *Write/flush/replay lag*, *Replication diff bytes*, *Retained-WAL slots*, *Replica WAL receivers up*, *Active slots*. (Populated on **stable**.)
- **➖ Empty on develop only — no backups configured:** *Time since last backup*, *First recoverability point*, *WAL archived vs failed*, *Pending WAL to archive*. (Populated on **stable**.)
- **📈 Need traffic to be non-zero** (idle DB → flat): *Commits/TPS*, *Tuple operations*, *Temp files & spill*, *WAL bytes/records*, *Checkpoints*, *Connections*. Generate load with the **`Day2.traffic.01-k6`** workflow (or any app traffic).

> So on **stable** under load, essentially every data-bearing panel populates and the rest read `0` because the cluster is **healthy**. On **develop**, additionally the Replication and Backups sections stay empty — that is the lean tier, not a bug.

panels.title: Instances Up
panels.description: cnpg_collector_up — collector reachable per instance.

panels.title: Primaries
panels.description: Instances NOT in recovery (= primary). One per CNPG cluster.

panels.title: Streaming replicas
panels.description: Replicas actively streaming from the primary.

panels.title: PostgreSQL version
panels.description: cnpg_collector_postgres_version.

panels.title: Oldest uptime
panels.description: Time since the earliest postmaster start.

panels.title: Connections
panels.description: Total backends across selected instances.

panels.title: Total data size
panels.description: Sum of all selected database sizes.

panels.title: Max replication lag
panels.description: Worst replica lag (seconds).

panels.title: Switchover required
panels.description: A manual switchover is pending.

panels.title: Fencing
panels.description: Instance fencing engaged.

panels.title: Collector errors /s
panels.description: cnpg_errors_total rate (0 when the metric has never fired).

panels.title: Sync replicas
panels.description: Configured synchronous replicas.

panels.title: Connections by state
panels.description: Backends grouped by state (active / idle / idle in transaction …).

panels.title: Backends waiting (on a lock)
panels.description: Sessions blocked waiting for a lock.

panels.title: Longest running transaction
panels.description: Age of the oldest open transaction — long txns block vacuum & hold XID.

panels.title: Temp files & spill /s
panels.description: Work that spilled to disk (raise work_mem if high).

panels.title: Cache hit ratio
panels.description: Shared-buffer hit ratio; <99% suggests memory pressure.

panels.title: Block read time /s
panels.description: Time spent reading blocks from disk.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (datname) (rate(cnpg_pg_stat_database_blks_hit_total{namespace="$namespace",datname=~"$datname"}[$__rate_interval])) / clamp_min(sum by (datname) (rate(cnpg_pg_stat_database_blks_hit_total{namespace="$namespace",datname=~"$datname"}[$__rate_interval])) + sum by (datname) (rate(cnpg_pg_stat_database_blks_read_total{namespace="$namespace",datname=~"$datname"}[$__rate_interval])), 1)

panels.title: WAL buffers full /s
panels.description: Backends flushing WAL because wal_buffers filled — consider raising wal_buffers.

panels.title: Checkpoints /s (timed vs requested)
panels.description: Many 'requested' checkpoints ⇒ raise max_wal_size.

panels.title: Replication (streaming) — primary has no replicas on single-instance tiers (e.g. develop)
Write / flush / replay lag (seconds)
panels.description: 0 on single-instance clusters (no streaming replicas).

panels.title: Replication diff bytes (primary→replica pipeline)
panels.description: 0 on single-instance clusters (no streaming replicas).

panels.title: Replication slots — retained WAL
panels.description: WAL retained for slots — unbounded growth = stuck/inactive consumer. 0 without replicas.

panels.title: Replica WAL receivers up
panels.description: Count of replicas with their WAL receiver streaming. 0 on single-instance tiers (e.g. develop). Uses sum() so the primary (which has no receiver) doesn't mask healthy replicas.

panels.title: Transaction-ID (XID) age
panels.description: Age toward the ~2.1B wraparound horizon (autovacuum_freeze_max_age). Climbing = vacuum not keeping up.

panels.title: Max XID age (% to wraparound)
panels.description: Distance to the 2^31 wraparound limit.

panels.title: Time since last successful backup
panels.description: cnpg_collector_last_available_backup_timestamp. Shows 'no backups configured' until a Barman/object-store backup runs.

panels.title: First recoverability point
panels.description: Earliest PITR target (needs backups).

panels.title: Pending WAL to archive
panels.description: WAL segments waiting to be archived (.ready files).

panels.title: Postgres / CNPG logs — selected instances
panels.description: Filter by $level (default All). Click a line to expand fields.

targets.expr: {k8s_namespace_name="$namespace", k8s_pod_name=~"$pod"} | detected_level=~"$level" | json | line_format "{{ if .message }}{{ .message }}{{ else if .msg }}{{ .msg }}{{ else }}{{ __line__ }}{{ end }}"

list.name: level
list.label: Log level
list.description: Filter the logs panel by severity (detected_level). All = everything incl. plain-text (unknown).
